one_time_description: Industry grants and other revenue of $13,445,518 appears non-recurring (vs $132,487 prior year); also Settlement Funds expenses of $2,494,281 with no prior year equivalent
economic_notes: The First Nation has investments in ACFN Business Trust (total assets $91.7M, net assets $51.6M), 2160415 Alberta Ltd., and 2216328 Alberta Ltd. Income from joint venture and partnership was $3,567,664. Investment in Cree Chip Development Corporation at $2,639,350 (50% owned). COVID-19 pandemic noted as uncertainty for future periods.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Consolidated entity includes 13 segments plus 4 GBEs accounted for by modified equity method (Acden Business Trust, ACFN Business Trust, 2160415 Alberta Ltd., 2216328 Alberta Ltd.). Accumulated surplus on balance sheet is $136,755,789 (excluding remeasurement losses of $6,308,408 giving total of $130,447,381). Industry grants and other revenue spiked to $13.4M vs $132K prior year - likely non-recurring. Settlement Funds program had $2.49M expenses with no prior year equivalent. ACFN Business Trust paid $2,003,372 of expenses on behalf of First Nation and sold a building for $1,072,450 settled as non-cash distribution. Ottawa Trust Fund ($6,485) held in trust by Government of Canada. Long-term debt note appears truncated in source document. total_government_transfers includes ISC ($1,672,480), CMHC ($293,013), ESDC ($137,080), Province of Alberta ($77,970) and Nutrition North Canada ($337,985) = $2,518,528 rounded to $2,518,538 with minor rounding. COVID-19 uncertainty noted by management.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
